APPEARANCES- ATTY. NANCY RASCH APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;

MS. RASCH REQUESTED THE COURT FOR A CONTINUANCE TO BE ABLE TO FILE A CORRECTED INVENTORY. THE COURT CONTINUED THE MATTER TO APRIL 29, 2008. Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Leticia Perez, CSR 7782. Clerk: Cynthia Herbert. Bailiff: Deputy H. Harris.

Petition For Order Bringing Revocable Trust Under Court Supervision And Appointing Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Dec-21-2006 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Verna Shaheen (Temporary Conservator); Nancy Rasch (Counsel for Ms. Shaheen); Pauline Blanc (Proposed Conservatee); Gemma O'Keefe (Court-appointed Counsel for Ms. Blanc); John Blanc (Interested Person/Cousin); Sue Blanc (Interested Person/Cousin's Wife).

At the outset, the Court noted that the matter was procedurally in order and that no improprieties were found on the family's part. Ms. O'Keefe reported that Ms. Blanc had no objections to Ms. Shaheen's appointment as Conservator or as Successor Trustee. However, Ms. Blanc did express concerns regarding placement and accommodations. Ms. Rasch requested that Ms. Blanc receive a $500.00 monthly allowance in addition to maintaining her own checking account totaling up to $1000.00 per month. The Court granted her request for the same.

With regard to the trust, the Court questioned whether Ms. Adrienne Lacau had filed a withdrawal as Successor Trustee. Ms. Rasch reported that Ms. Lacau did not file a withdrawal, but did consent to Verna Shaheen's appointment. Consequently, the Court found it appropriate to appoint Verna Shaheen the Successor Trustee and the Conservator of the Person and Estate of Pauline Blanc (signing the Orders for same in open court). Ms. O'Keefe was discharged as Court-appointed Counsel for Ms. Blanc.

Note: See Case No. 289270 mini-minutes for 12/21/06 for Conservatorship dates.

Further Orders for Successor Trustee:
Inventory and Appraisal must be filed two weeks before: April 4, 2007.
First Account must be filed two weeks before: March 4, 2008.
Bond is set at $3,050,000.00.

Judicial Officer: Commissioner Dorothy L. McMath; Reporter: Karla Davis #12998; Court Investigator: John Norton; Bailiff: Mizota; Clerk: C. Joy Guandique.
